name: Disguised Ads
summary: Advertising dressed up as content, native UI elements, or editorial recommendations.

evidence.claim: Disguised ads is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy.
FTC Endorsement Guides require disclosure of material connections between endorsers and advertisers, and treat undisclosed sponsored content as deceptive.

body.description: The user believes they are clicking editorial content or a native UI element, but the link is paid placement. Common forms: 'sponsored' rows hidden in organic search results, affiliate links not marked, paid partnerships in review articles without disclosure (E1, E2).

detectionHeuristics.description: Many sites mark ads in DOM attributes even when visual distinction is minimal.
Outbound links containing affiliate codes (amzn.to, aff=, utm_medium=affiliate) indicate paid relationship.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given the page content and outbound link URLs, classify each product mention as (a) organic editorial, (b) clearly-disclosed sponsorship, or (c) undisclosed sponsored content. Check for affiliate parameters in outbound URLs.
body.remediation: Label each product mention with its sponsorship status. If undisclosed, warn the user and flag the source for downranking in future Lens source-authority weighting.
